title: Tuning a Drifting Pirate Radio
strategies: do-nothing baseline: spend zero probes, predict the fixed-shape/constant-loudness naive reading using only the midpoints of the public hint windows (reproduces the evaluator's zero-probe reference exactly)

single-snapshot sweep: spend the whole budget hunting for the station once near the start of the observed window, fit its shape and instantaneous position/loudness precisely, then extrapolate that STATIC fit unchanged into the future window -- captures the receiver's fixed shape but not either drift, so it is blindsided once the frequency has crept away and the loudness cycle has turned around

timescale-split allocation: a cheap shape fit riding along for free, a few widely time-separated anchor sweeps to regress the frequency's LINEAR drift, then the bulk of the remaining budget spent thinly across many ticks (one probe per tick, dialled onto the just-fitted moving peak) to read the loudness trace and fit its SINUSOID via a small grid search over candidate periods drawn from the public hint

regime-aware prediction: for every held-out query, evaluate the fitted linear frequency and harmonic loudness models at that future tick and apply the closed-form reception shape, instead of holding either quantity fixed at its last observed value
